primary_profiles.evidence: Repeated references to 'company leadership,' 'CEOs and their families,' 'executives,' board members. Tom Brokaw (former NBC anchor) cited as a client flight donation. CEO advice published in Chief Executive Magazine targets peer CEOs.

Explicit blog post titled 'In their words: Learn how a PlaneSense fractional share helps this CEO and his family.' Content on managing franchises across multiple California locations implies owner-operator profile.

Heavy content around vacation homes, family getaways, second homes in Nantucket, ski weekends in Colorado, beach homes in Bahamas and California. Pet travel content signals high-value lifestyle segment.

Dedicated URL slug '/who-flies-the-planesense-program-golfers/' referenced multiple times across destination posts. Destinations like Elk River Club (Jack Nicklaus course), Baker's Bay Golf & Ocean Club (Tom Fazio course), Fox Harbour golf featured.
primary_profiles.notes: Golf is a recurring cross-sell theme linking leisure flying to exclusive destination clubs.

primary_profiles.segment: Military Veterans Transitioning to Civil Aviation
primary_profiles.evidence: Dedicated career blog for military veterans at Atlas Aircraft Center. Multiple branches represented in workforce. Veterans explicitly targeted as prospective employees.
primary_profiles.notes: This is an employer brand segment, not a customer segment, but it signals the workforce culture PlaneSense projects to the market.

who_are_their_customers.geographic_base: Primarily Northeast US (New Hampshire HQ), expanding to West Coast (California, Nevada operations hub in Boulder City, NV near Las Vegas). National service coverage stated.
who_are_their_customers.company_size_indicators: Clients described as needing 35+ air hours per year to justify fractional ownership. Share sizes from 1/16 to whole ownership under Part 91K or Part 135.

what_customers_value_most.evidence: Dominant theme across all content. 'Fly on your schedule, no blackout dates.' 'Arrive minutes before takeoff.' 'Home in time for dinner.' Contrasted explicitly against commercial TSA lines, delays, and missed connections.

900+ airports accessible. 'Get closer to your destination' is the literal brand tagline. Short runway capability highlighted in every fleet article. Nantucket cited as the #1 summer destination, Bahamas, Telluride, private island resorts featured repeatedly.

'You will always know your flying costs and fees up front.' 'No repositioning or empty leg costs.' Costs 'nearly half the cost of comparable programs.' Fractional pitched as predictable vs. charter unpredictability.

ARGUS Platinum Elite Safety Status (one of only 5 US aviation companies). FAA Diamond Award for Excellence every year since 2002. Pilatus avionics: synthetic vision, weather radar, TCAS, flight path guidance cited.
'Complete privacy from take-off to touchdown.' Wi-Fi via Gogo AVANCE L3 fleet-wide since 2023. Quiet cabin. Flat floor design. Ability to hold confidential meetings en route.
3,696 pets flew in 2021 alone (21% increase over 2019). Flat floor, climate-controlled cargo area, six large seats. Pets referenced as 'members of the family.'
'No blackout dates.' 'Book a flight with only hours' notice.' 24/7/365 Flight Operations Center.

business_vs_leisure_split.assessment: Approximately 50/50 blend, with the marketing narrative carefully serving both use cases simultaneously.
business_signals: Content on productivity, confidential meetings, last-minute travel
Tax benefit messaging (100% bonus depreciation in Year 1)
Airport access: Teterboro NJ, DeKalb-Peachtree Atlanta, White Plains NY highlighted for business hub avoidance
Articles on 'return to business travel' post-COVID
leisure_signals: Nearly all featured destination content is leisure: Nantucket, Bahamas, Bermuda, Telluride, Fredericksburg TX, Coastal New England islands
Summer Nantucket has nearly 500 trips in peak season alone
Pet travel data is a leisure signal
Fly-in resorts (Wyoming horseback, Nova Scotia golf, Texas rafting) featured in Business Jet Traveler magazine partnership
Vacation homes, ski weekends, beach getaways referenced constantly

customer_retention_signals: Phrase 'PlaneSense for more than 23-25 years' — emphasizing multi-decade loyalty of owners
Families returning to same beaches 'year after year' as a content theme
Nantucket: nearly 500 trips in one short summer season from repeat owners
Program grew through COVID while competitors contracted — no layoffs mentioned, implying stable owner base
Parts team grew from 2 people to 9 people (7 days/week) as a retention infrastructure signal
Framing of share owners as 'family members' not customers
CobaltPass jet card introduced as a feeder/loyalty product for those not ready for fractional ownership

content_topics_frequency.topic: Fleet capabilities and differentiators (PC-12 and PC-24)
content_topics_frequency.notes: Almost every piece of content ties back to short runway access, cabin size, Swiss engineering, fuel efficiency, or safety features.
Largest content category. Islands, ski resorts, golf clubs, wine country, coastal New England dominate. Content published 2017-2025.

content_topics_frequency.topic: Fractional ownership education (FAR Part 91K vs 135, cost, share sizes)
content_topics_frequency.notes: SEO-driven educational content. Topics: 'what is fractional ownership,' 'myths about fractional travel,' '10 questions to ask,' 'cost of fractional ownership.'
ARGUS Platinum Elite, FAA Diamond Award, COVID safety protocols, Atlas Aircraft Center standards.
ROI of private aviation, return to business travel, confidential meetings, flexible scheduling.
Parts department (194,000 parts inventory), hangar timelapse, aircraft ferry flights from Switzerland, crew culture.
30 years of innovation (2025), 2,000th PC-12 delivery, Gogo Wi-Fi installation, Jetfly partnership, simulator launch, CEO awards.
Military veteran career stories, pilot retention, A&P mechanic hiring, talent retention articles.
Gogo AVANCE L3 (Wi-Fi), Gozen Digital Aviation (scheduling/Operator platform). Explicit digital aviation focus.

channels: HubSpot CRM for lead capture (info.planesense.com CTAs)
content_marketing_approach.seo_strategy: Heavy use of destination names as long-tail keywords. Aircraft model names (PC-12, PC-24) with use-case descriptors. FAR regulatory terms for informed buyer capture.
content_marketing_approach.lead_generation: HubSpot-powered forms on every page. CTA: 'Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution.' Phone: 866-214-1212. Email: sales@planesense.com.
content_marketing_approach.publication_cadence: Regular blog posts from 2015 through 2025. Most recent content published 2025-2026 (Nantucket Aug 2025, 30 Years celebration Sep 2025, CEO award Feb 2026).
content_marketing_approach.social_proof: Minimal customer testimonials in scraped content; one CEO testimonial URL referenced. Relies more on 3rd party awards (ARGUS, FAA Diamond) than customer reviews.

competitive_positioning.vs_whole_aircraft_ownership: Explicitly positioned as smarter, more cost-effective. 'Founded on the belief there is a smarter way to fly than whole aircraft ownership.' Eliminates hangar fees, maintenance management, pilot scheduling.
competitive_positioning.vs_charter: Positions charter as 'unpredictable.' 'No repositioning or empty leg costs like you might with charter.' 'Personalized service vs unpredictable charter services.'
competitive_positioning.vs_jet_cards: Positions jet cards as less reliable. 'Frustrated with charter and jet card options.' CobaltPass jet card introduced as a secondary product (suggesting they also serve the jet card market now).
competitive_positioning.vs_other_fractional_programs: Price: 'Nearly half the cost of comparable programs.' Airport access: 'Four times more airports.' Fleet age: '4.5 years average' vs competitors. No explicit naming of competitors (NetJets, Flexjet, Wheels Up).
competitive_positioning.pc_24_vs_phenom_300: Explicit comparison article 'Pilatus PC-24 vs Phenom 300: 9 Reasons the PC-24 Wins' (Sep 2025) — indicates Embraer Phenom 300 is the primary direct competitor in the PC-24 segment.

key_marketing_messages: Get closer to your destination (short runway access = more airports)
The sensible private travel solution (cost-efficiency over luxury excess)
Swiss engineering, quiet luxury (Pilatus premium positioning)
Fly on your schedule, not someone else's
Nearly half the cost of comparable programs
The largest civilian fleet of Pilatus PC-12 and PC-24 in the world
30 years of innovation and trust (30th anniversary milestone in 2025)
Safety first: ARGUS Platinum Elite, FAA Diamond Award since 2002

confirmed_international_destinations: Bahamas (Staniel Cay, Baker's Bay, Cat Cay — PC-12 on 2,000' crushed coral runway)
Central America (mentioned in Fredericksburg TX article)
Europe (via Jetfly partnership — see E: Hidden Intelligence)

airports_served_metrics.recent_claim_2025: More than 1,250 airports in the last year (from Fredericksburg TX article, published May 2025)

leisure_island_retreats: Nantucket, MA (top summer destination, ~500 trips per season)
Baker's Bay Golf & Ocean Club, Bahamas
ski_and_mountain_resorts: Telluride, CO (PC-24 featured landing at TEX)
Jackson Hole, WY (image reference: Benjamin Pardini Jackson WY)
coastal_new_england: Bar Harbor, ME (Hancock County-Bar Harbor Airport BHB)
North Carolina Outer Banks (First Flight Airport, Billy Mitchell Airport, Ocracoke Airport)
golf_destinations: Elk River Club, Banner Elk, NC (Jack Nicklaus course, 4,600' private runway)
Baker's Bay Golf & Ocean Club, Bahamas (Tom Fazio course)
Fox Harbour Golf & Spa Resort, Nova Scotia
wine_and_culinary: Fredericksburg, TX (Texas Wine Country, 75+ wineries, Gillespie County Airport)
Inn at Little Washington, VA (Luray Caverns Airport LUA)
adventure_and_motorsports: Barber Motorsports Park, Birmingham, AL (Porsche Driving School)
urban_business_hubs_avoided_or_alternati: Teterboro, NJ (TEB) instead of JFK/LGA for NYC
DeKalb-Peachtree, GA (PDK) instead of ATL for Atlanta
Midland, TX (cited as under-served by commercial)

airport_access_advantages.vs_commercial_airlines: Commercial airlines serve ~500 US airports; PlaneSense claims access to 4x more
notable_short_field_ops: Fishers Island, NY / Elizabeth Field Airport (0B8): 2,345' runway — one of the only fractional programs with access
Provincetown, MA (KPVC): 3,500' with PC-24 well before end of runway
Luray Caverns Airport, VA: short runway accessible by PC-12 and PC-24
Gillespie County Airport, TX: 3 miles from Fredericksburg downtown
Elk River Club, NC: 4,600' private runway on-site

route_density_signals.nantucket_summer: Nearly 500 trips in the short summer season — by far the highest density single-destination route mentioned
route_density_signals.new_england_corridor: Dominant corridor. CT, MA, RI, ME, NH islands mentioned across multiple articles. HQ in Portsmouth NH creates natural density
route_density_signals.west_coast_expansion: California (LAX area, SFO area, San Diego, Napa, Lake Tahoe, Big Bear) and Nevada (Boulder City/Las Vegas ops hub) now part of service area. Articles from 2022 onward confirm West Coast active.
route_density_signals.florida_mentions: Southern Florida PC-12 image used. Bahamas as extension of Florida market.
route_density_signals.mid_atlantic: DC area (Luray VA), NJ shore (Cape May), NYC metro suburbs (Teterboro, White Plains)

historical_data_points: Veteran employee blog (2023): references 'over 75 Pilatus aircraft' historically
Fleet size appears to have contracted or stabilized around 46 aircraft in 2023

fleet_age.upgrade_policy: Aircraft cycled out after 10-12 years. Described as deliberately keeping fleet young.
fleet_age.newest_aircraft: PC-12 NGX (NGX is the latest variant, references to NGX in 2024-2025 articles suggest recent NGX fleet additions)

seasonal_utilization.peak_summer: Nantucket alone generates ~500 trips in a short summer season. Coastal New England and island destinations create clear summer demand spike.
seasonal_utilization.ski_season: Telluride, Colorado, Jackson Hole referenced in winter context. High-altitude airport performance is a marketing feature addressing this segment.
seasonal_utilization.year_round_claim: PlaneSense explicitly markets 'year round' New England flying and national service. 'No blackout dates' as a core promise.
seasonal_utilization.demand_signals: 3,696 pets in 2021 (21% above 2019). 37,300 missions in 2021. 918 airports in 2017.

capacity_and_availability.guaranteed_availability_promise: Core product promise. No blackout dates. Book with hours notice.
capacity_and_availability.capacity_challenges_mentioned: No explicit capacity complaints mentioned. However, article 'Avoiding the Aircraft Shortage with Fractional Aircraft Ownership' URL slug detected in internal links — implies awareness of industry-wide aircraft availability constraints post-2020.
capacity_and_availability.covid_response: Company grew through COVID while others contracted. No layoffs. Continued hiring and expansion cited as proof of financial resilience.
capacity_and_availability.new_product_signal: CobaltPass jet card launched as a new program — likely serves overflow demand or lower-commitment customers who cannot get fractional allocation

facilities: Portsmouth, NH (primary — on former Pease AFB)
Boulder City, NV (near Las Vegas — Western operations hub)
maintenance_and_infrastructure.parts_inventory: 194,000+ Pilatus aircraft parts — one of the largest inventories in the US
certifications: FAA Diamond Award for Excellence — every year since 2002 (Atlas Aircraft Center)
Avionics Training Excellence Award from Aircraft Electronics Association (AEA) — multiple years
ARGUS Platinum Elite Safety Status — one of only 5 US aviation companies
maintenance_and_infrastructure.staffing: Parts team grew from 2 to 9 people on two shifts, 7 days/week. Overnight preparation teams. 24/7 Flight Operations Center.
maintenance_and_infrastructure.parts_team_catering: On-board catering managed by Parts team — stocked at multiple locations across the service area

simulator_and_training.major_milestone: PlaneSense Launches World's First Pilatus PC-12 NGX Level D Full Motion Flight Simulator (announced February 24, 2025 — ribbon cutting held)
simulator_and_training.significance: Level D is the highest FAA certification level for full-motion flight simulators. This is globally unprecedented for the PC-12 NGX specifically.
simulator_and_training.training_implications: Enables in-house pilot training and proficiency checking without ferry flights or reliance on external simulators. Cost savings and regulatory compliance (Part 91K/135). Potential revenue as a training center for Pilatus operators globally.
simulator_and_training.prior_simulator: Older Pilatus PC-12 simulator referenced in veteran blog photo (PSI-Simulator-1024x682.jpg) suggesting prior simulator capability

connectivity_upgrades.gogo_avance_l3: Fleet-wide installation across PC-12s and PC-24s. Jet fleet had L3 since 2018. PC-12 rollout completed 2023.
connectivity_upgrades.features: Full internet, Gogo Vision moving map, starts at 3,000 feet AGL
connectivity_upgrades.managed_by: Atlas Aircraft Center handled all installations

strategic_moves_hinted.detail: January 22, 2025: PlaneSense and Jetfly (European fractional/charter operator) formally partner. Separate landing page at planesense.com/europe/ with Jetfly branding. Jetfly fleet mentioned. Countries covered: Europe and North African nations. This gives PlaneSense clients seamless access to European travel for the first time — a major geographic expansion without capital outlay.
strategic_moves_hinted.strategic_implication: Network effect product launch. Enables upsell/cross-sell to existing owners traveling to Europe. Positions PlaneSense as a global private aviation solution. Opens door to European client acquisition partnerships.

strategic_moves_hinted.detail: New product introduced in 2024-2025. Separate URL (/program-options/cobaltpass-jet-card/). Allows customizable hour blocks with guaranteed availability, no hidden fees. Referenced in Fredericksburg TX article (May 2025).
strategic_moves_hinted.strategic_implication: Lower entry-point product targeting prospects not ready for fractional ownership. Expands total addressable market. Also useful as a business development tool for prospective fractional owners to 'try before they buy.'

strategic_moves_hinted.detail: Boulder City, NV maintenance hub (second major facility). Multiple articles from 2022+ dedicated to West Coast flying. Internal link URL: /planesense-inc-to-offer-fractional-jet-shares-to-the-west-coast/ confirms this was an announced expansion.
strategic_moves_hinted.strategic_implication: Significant capital and operational expansion westward. New owner recruitment in CA, NV, AZ markets. Competes directly with West Coast fractional operators.

strategic_moves_hinted.move: World's First PC-12 NGX Level D Full Motion Simulator
strategic_moves_hinted.detail: Launched February 24, 2025. Ribbon-cutting event held at Portsmouth HQ. Global first for this aircraft variant.
strategic_moves_hinted.strategic_implication: Potential revenue center (training other Pilatus PC-12 operators worldwide). Competitive moat in pilot training. Regulatory efficiency for Part 91K/135 compliance. Positions PlaneSense as a training thought leader in the Pilatus ecosystem.

strategic_moves_hinted.detail: October 2023 — Extended collaboration with Gozen Digital Aviation (Istanbul-based). Platform: 'Operator' — a web-based flight scheduling tool integrating schedule planning, trip preparation, and disruption recovery. CEO quoted: 'We are hopeful that our cooperation will grow and enhance other aspects of the PlaneSense operations.'
strategic_moves_hinted.strategic_implication: Digital transformation of flight operations scheduling. Suggests PlaneSense is investing in operational AI/automation beyond basic software. Gozen Group has 40+ years in aviation catering; this is a technology pivot partner. Open door for Calfus engagement on AI-driven operational optimization.

strategic_moves_hinted.detail: September 9, 2025: 'PlaneSense Celebrates 30 Years of Innovation.' September 30, 2025: 'Elevating Private Flying: 30 years of PlaneSense Innovation.' Business Jet Traveler (BJT) cover story confirmed (image URL: BJT_Cover_Photo-1024x576.jpg).
strategic_moves_hinted.strategic_implication: Legacy brand assertion. Likely accompanied by investor/owner communications, premium pricing validation, and new product launches riding the milestone momentum.

strategic_moves_hinted.detail: February 4, 2026: George Antoniadis awarded the Kenn Ricci Lifetime Aviation Entrepreneur Award at Living Legends of Aviation event. Photo published 2026-02-24 (same day as scrape). Antoniadis also featured in Chief Executive Magazine (March 2020).
strategic_moves_hinted.strategic_implication: CEO is the public face of the brand. High industry credibility. Kenn Ricci (founder of Directional Aviation/Flexjet) award signals Antoniadis is now peer-level with the biggest names in fractional aviation. Likely elevates PlaneSense's position in industry M&A conversations.

strategic_moves_hinted.detail: October 4, 2024 article with URL /planesense-ranked-as-1-private-lift-provider/. Source of ranking not confirmed in scraped data but indicates third-party validation.
strategic_moves_hinted.strategic_implication: Market leadership claim in private lift. Useful for customer acquisition and retention messaging.

regulatory_and_compliance_mentions.context: Fractional ownership programs governed under Part 91K. Share sizes minimum 1/16. Operations compared vs Part 135. PlaneSense defaults to 91K unless owner's insurance or other factors require 135.
regulatory_and_compliance_mentions.implication: Regulatory complexity is a barrier to entry the company uses as a competitive moat and content marketing topic.

regulatory_and_compliance_mentions.context: Some fractional clients operate under Part 135 (charter-equivalent rules) depending on insurance requirements or share size.
regulatory_and_compliance_mentions.implication: Dual regulatory compliance is an operational complexity requiring sophisticated scheduling and crew management systems.

regulatory_and_compliance_mentions.context: Atlas Aircraft Center has received this every year since 2002.
regulatory_and_compliance_mentions.implication: Training excellence certification — supports the simulator investment rationale.

regulatory_and_compliance_mentions.context: One of only 5 aviation companies in the US to hold this.
regulatory_and_compliance_mentions.implication: Highest third-party safety audit rating. Used in sales and marketing materials.

regulatory_and_compliance_mentions.context: PC-12 NGX Level D simulator launched Feb 2025. Level D is the highest FAA simulator certification.
regulatory_and_compliance_mentions.implication: Enables recurrent training and initial type qualification in-house. Reduces dependency on Pilatus or third-party training centers.

regulatory_and_compliance_mentions.context: Fleet content mentions curfew adherence and noise reduction measures near airport communities.
regulatory_and_compliance_mentions.implication: Community relations and noise regulation compliance as part of operations strategy.

industry_trends_they_are_responding_to.trend: Commercial airline service reduction to secondary markets
industry_trends_they_are_responding_to.response: Repeated content about airlines cutting routes (Midland TX, smaller cities). Positions PlaneSense as the solution when commercial options disappear.

industry_trends_they_are_responding_to.trend: Post-COVID demand surge for private aviation
industry_trends_they_are_responding_to.response: COVID content pivot from 'safety during pandemic' to 'return to business travel.' Fleet grew rather than contracted. Pet flights +21% in 2021 vs 2019.
Gogo AVANCE L3 fleet-wide by 2023. CEO quoted: 'Clients greatly enjoyed Gogo connectivity since 2018 on jets, now offering to PC-12 clients.' WiFi presented as table-stakes productivity tool.
Gozen Digital Aviation 'Operator' platform for complex fractional scheduling. CEO mentioned: 'tens of thousands of fractional operations' requiring digital coordination.

industry_trends_they_are_responding_to.trend: Aircraft shortage and supply chain constraints
industry_trends_they_are_responding_to.response: URL slug 'avoiding-the-aircraft-shortage-with-fractional-aircraft-ownership' detected. CobaltPass jet card may partly address capacity constraints.
industry_trends_they_are_responding_to.fleet_strategy: Continuous cycle of new Pilatus aircraft (5-7 year average age), ensuring clients always have access to modern avionics and safety systems.

industry_trends_they_are_responding_to.trend: European private aviation demand from US clients
industry_trends_they_are_responding_to.response: Jetfly partnership January 2025 — direct response to owner demand for European travel capability.
Explicit September 2025 article: 'Pilatus PC-24 vs Phenom 300: 9 Reasons the PC-24 Wins.' Demonstrates awareness of Embraer as primary aircraft competitor in the light jet segment.

metrics_and_statistics_confirmed.source: Coastal New England destination article, August 2024

metrics_and_statistics_confirmed.metric: PC-12 fleet size as of Feb 2023

metrics_and_statistics_confirmed.value: 32 PC-12s + all 7 PC-24s with active AVANCE L3 as of Feb 2023

metrics_and_statistics_confirmed.metric: Number of PC-12s ever delivered to PlaneSense
metrics_and_statistics_confirmed.value: 65th delivered in early 2019; 69th in production mid-2019. 2,000th PC-12 built by Pilatus was delivered to PlaneSense.
Prices nearly half the cost of comparable programs

CALFUS_OPPORTUNITY_MAP.note: This section maps PlaneSense signals to Calfus service lines as conversation starters for the CXO meeting.

opportunities.signal: Gozen Digital Aviation 'Operator' partnership for complex scheduling. CEO said 'we are hopeful our cooperation will grow.' 37,300+ missions/year requiring coordination.
opportunities.calfus_angle: Calfus AI Agents could augment or replace legacy scheduling logic with intelligent, self-optimizing allocation — handling disruption recovery, aircraft repositioning optimization, and crew scheduling with real-time data. This directly extends the vision Antoniadis expressed.

opportunities.signal: 194,000 parts inventory. Two maintenance facilities. FAA Diamond Award culture. Parts team on two shifts, 7 days/week. Fleet age management is a deliberate strategy (cycling out aircraft at 10-12 years).
opportunities.calfus_angle: AI-driven predictive maintenance scheduling, parts demand forecasting, and inventory optimization (integrated with their likely ERP backend). Atlas Aircraft Center is the natural entry point.

opportunities.signal: Company has grown from small club to 46-aircraft, 2-facility operation over 30 years. Parts tracking, crew scheduling, maintenance tracking, owner billing, and catering management are all described as separate systems.
opportunities.calfus_angle: Calfus Oracle Cloud practice — consolidating fragmented operational systems (parts/inventory, billing, crew management, catering) into an integrated platform. 30-year-old company likely running legacy systems.

opportunities.signal: Rich behavioral data: 918+ airports served, 37,300+ annual missions, seasonal spikes (Nantucket 500 trips/summer), pet travel data, multi-year customer tenure. HubSpot CRM in use for lead management.
opportunities.calfus_angle: Calfus Data & Analytics — building a customer intelligence platform on top of flight data, CRM, and booking patterns to enable personalized owner communications, churn prediction, and dynamic pricing.

opportunities.signal: New Jetfly European partnership (January 2025). Two separate operator systems (US and EU) now need to present a seamless client experience.
opportunities.calfus_angle: API integration layer between PlaneSense and Jetfly scheduling/booking systems. Digital transformation of the cross-network client experience. Potential joint CRM/data platform for cross-Atlantic owner insights.

opportunities.signal: New CobaltPass product requires dynamic pricing, hours allocation, and availability management.
opportunities.calfus_angle: AI agents for real-time jet card inventory management, demand forecasting, and automated client communication — similar to airline revenue management but purpose-built for fractional/card hybrid models.

opportunities.signal: World's first PC-12 NGX Level D simulator launched February 2025. Potential to become a training center for other Pilatus operators globally.
opportunities.calfus_angle: Digital platform for simulator scheduling, third-party client booking, training records management, and FAA compliance documentation — positioning Atlas/PlaneSense as a commercial training center.
